mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate signals an accommodative monetary policy that lowers discount rates for equity valuations, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A monthly decline in the unemployment rate below 3.5% indicates tightening labor markets, which drives corporate profit growth and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 5-day returns in 60%+ of instances where UNRATE falls below 3.5%.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in residential construction (HOUST) and consumer spending (RSAFS) signals strong demand conditions, creating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint growth of HOUST and RSAFS fails to produce positive 60-day returns in 50%+ of cases, or their correlation drops below 0.4.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly decline in industrial production (INDPRO) indicates supply-side disruptions, which increase inflationary pressures and generate negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in 60%+ of instances where INDPRO declines.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly increase in nonfarm payrolls (PAYEMS) above 150k signals robust labor market conditions, driving corporate earnings growth and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 5-day returns in 50%+ of instances where PAYEMS exceeds 150k.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly CPI inflation reading above 2.5% triggers central bank hawkishness, leading to higher real rates and negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in 60%+ of instances where CPIAUCSL exceeds 2.5%.

mechanism_narrative: A quarterly GDP growth rate above 3% indicates strong economic momentum, which drives equity risk appetite and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 60-day returns in 50%+ of instances where GDPC1 exceeds 3%.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in housing starts (HOUST) and consumer spending (RSAFS) signals resilient demand, creating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint growth of HOUST and RSAFS fails to produce positive 20-day returns in 50%+ of cases, or their correlation drops below 0.3.
